questions.question: Who wrote 'Charlie and the Chocolate Factory'?
questions.explanation: Roald Dahl wrote 'Charlie and the Chocolate Factory'. He was a British author famous for his imaginative children's books.
questions.funFact: Roald Dahl was also a spy and a fighter pilot before becoming a writer!

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Cat in the Hat'?
questions.explanation: Dr. Seuss wrote 'The Cat in the Hat'. His real name was Theodor Seuss Geisel, and he wrote many beloved rhyming books for children.
questions.funFact: Dr. Seuss wrote 'The Cat in the Hat' using only 236 different words!

questions.question: Which book was written by Eric Carle?
questions.explanation: Eric Carle wrote and illustrated 'The Very Hungry Caterpillar'. It is one of the best-selling children's picture books of all time.
questions.funFact: Eric Carle made the pictures in his books by cutting and pasting colourful tissue paper!

questions.question: Ruskin Bond is famous for writing which book?
questions.explanation: Ruskin Bond wrote 'The Blue Umbrella', a story about a little girl named Binya and her beautiful blue umbrella in the hills of India.
questions.funFact: Ruskin Bond has lived in the hills of Mussoorie, India, for over 50 years and loves writing about nature!

questions.question: Who wrote the 'Famous Five' series?
questions.explanation: Enid Blyton wrote the 'Famous Five' series about a group of children and their dog who go on exciting adventures.
questions.funFact: Enid Blyton wrote over 700 books in her lifetime, which is more than most authors ever write!

questions.question: Who wrote 'Green Eggs and Ham'?
questions.explanation: Dr. Seuss wrote 'Green Eggs and Ham'. It is a fun rhyming story about trying new foods.
questions.funFact: Dr. Seuss wrote 'Green Eggs and Ham' using only 50 different words because of a bet with his publisher!

questions.question: Who created the character Winnie-the-Pooh?
questions.explanation: A.A. Milne created Winnie-the-Pooh. The character was inspired by a teddy bear owned by his son, Christopher Robin.
questions.funFact: The real Winnie was a bear at the London Zoo that Christopher Robin loved to visit!

questions.question: Which author wrote 'The Tale of Peter Rabbit'?
questions.explanation: Beatrix Potter wrote and illustrated 'The Tale of Peter Rabbit', a story about a naughty little rabbit who sneaks into Mr. McGregor's garden.
questions.funFact: Beatrix Potter had a real pet rabbit named Peter that she used to take for walks on a leash!

questions.question: Roald Dahl wrote which of these books?
questions.explanation: Roald Dahl wrote 'Matilda', the story of a brilliant little girl with magical powers who loves reading books.
questions.funFact: Roald Dahl always wrote in a special shed in his garden, sitting in an old armchair with a board across his lap!

questions.question: Who wrote 'Where the Wild Things Are'?
questions.explanation: Maurice Sendak wrote and illustrated 'Where the Wild Things Are', a story about a boy named Max who sails to an island of wild creatures.
questions.funFact: When the book first came out, some grown-ups thought it was too scary for kids, but children absolutely loved it!

questions.question: Which author wrote stories about Mowgli, a boy raised by wolves?
questions.explanation: Rudyard Kipling wrote 'The Jungle Book' which has stories about Mowgli, a boy raised by wolves in the Indian jungle.
questions.funFact: Rudyard Kipling was born in Mumbai, India, and many of his stories are set in the jungles of India!

questions.question: Who wrote 'James and the Giant Peach'?
questions.explanation: Roald Dahl wrote 'James and the Giant Peach', an adventure about a boy who travels inside an enormous magical peach.
questions.funFact: Roald Dahl's favourite food was chocolate, which is why he wrote a whole book about a chocolate factory!

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Room on the Roof'?
questions.explanation: Ruskin Bond wrote 'The Room on the Roof' when he was just 17 years old. It is set in the town of Dehradun in India.
questions.funFact: Ruskin Bond won his first literary prize for 'The Room on the Roof' when he was only 21 years old!

questions.question: Enid Blyton wrote which of these adventure series?
questions.explanation: Enid Blyton wrote 'The Secret Seven' series about a group of seven children who solve mysteries together.
questions.funFact: Enid Blyton could type so fast that she sometimes finished an entire book in just five days!

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Gruffalo'?
questions.explanation: Julia Donaldson wrote 'The Gruffalo', a clever rhyming story about a mouse who invents a scary creature to protect itself.
questions.funFact: Julia Donaldson based 'The Gruffalo' on an ancient Chinese story about a fox who tricks a tiger!

questions.question: Who wrote 'Totto-Chan: The Little Girl at the Window'?
questions.explanation: Tetsuko Kuroyanagi wrote 'Totto-Chan', based on her own childhood experiences at a very special school in Japan.
questions.funFact: The school in the book used old train carriages as classrooms, and the children could choose what subject to study first each day!

questions.question: Which author created the 'Curious George' stories?
questions.explanation: H.A. Rey and his wife Margret Rey created the 'Curious George' stories about a mischievous little monkey.
questions.funFact: H.A. Rey and Margret escaped Paris on bicycles during World War II, carrying the Curious George manuscript with them!

questions.question: Sudha Murty is known for writing which type of children's books?
questions.explanation: Sudha Murty is a beloved Indian author known for books like 'Grandma's Bag of Stories' that retell Indian folktales for children.
questions.funFact: Sudha Murty was the first female engineer hired at TELCO (now Tata Motors) in India!

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Giving Tree'?
questions.explanation: Shel Silverstein wrote 'The Giving Tree', a touching story about the friendship between a boy and a tree.
questions.funFact: Shel Silverstein was also a cartoonist, songwriter, and poet. He even wrote a song that became a number one hit!

questions.question: Who wrote 'One Fish Two Fish Red Fish Blue Fish'?
questions.explanation: Dr. Seuss wrote 'One Fish Two Fish Red Fish Blue Fish', a fun and silly rhyming book that helps children learn to read.
questions.funFact: Dr. Seuss drew all his own illustrations, and his characters like the Lorax and the Grinch have become world-famous!

questions.question: Who wrote the Harry Potter series?
questions.explanation: J.K. Rowling wrote the Harry Potter series, one of the best-selling book series in history, about a boy wizard and his adventures at Hogwarts.
questions.funFact: J.K. Rowling came up with the idea for Harry Potter while sitting on a delayed train from Manchester to London!

questions.question: R.K. Narayan is famous for creating which fictional town?
questions.explanation: R.K. Narayan created the fictional town of Malgudi, which appears in many of his stories including 'Swami and Friends' and 'Malgudi Days'.
questions.funFact: R.K. Narayan's brother R.K. Laxman was a famous cartoonist who created the character 'Common Man'!

questions.question: Who wrote 'Diary of a Wimpy Kid'?
questions.explanation: Jeff Kinney wrote the 'Diary of a Wimpy Kid' series, funny illustrated novels about the life of middle schooler Greg Heffley.
questions.funFact: Jeff Kinney spent eight years working on the first Diary of a Wimpy Kid book before it was published!

questions.question: Who wrote the 'Percy Jackson' series?
questions.explanation: Rick Riordan wrote the 'Percy Jackson and the Olympians' series about a boy who discovers he is the son of a Greek god.
questions.funFact: Rick Riordan created Percy Jackson as bedtime stories for his son, who has ADHD and dyslexia, just like Percy!

questions.question: Which book did Lewis Carroll write?
questions.explanation: Lewis Carroll wrote 'Alice's Adventures in Wonderland', a fantastical story about a girl who falls down a rabbit hole into a strange world.
questions.funFact: Lewis Carroll was actually a mathematics teacher at Oxford University whose real name was Charles Lutwidge Dodgson!

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Chronicles of Narnia'?
questions.explanation: C.S. Lewis wrote 'The Chronicles of Narnia', a series of seven fantasy novels set in the magical land of Narnia.
questions.funFact: C.S. Lewis and J.R.R. Tolkien (who wrote 'The Lord of the Rings') were close friends and used to read each other's stories!

questions.question: Who wrote 'Swami and Friends'?
questions.explanation: R.K. Narayan wrote 'Swami and Friends', a charming novel about a boy named Swaminathan and his friends in the fictional town of Malgudi.
questions.funFact: The famous English author Graham Greene helped R.K. Narayan get 'Swami and Friends' published in England!

questions.question: Who wrote the 'Goosebumps' series?
questions.explanation: R.L. Stine wrote the 'Goosebumps' series, a collection of spooky and thrilling stories for young readers.
questions.funFact: R.L. Stine has written over 300 books and is sometimes called the Stephen King of children's literature!

questions.question: Rudyard Kipling wrote which of these stories?
questions.explanation: Rudyard Kipling wrote 'The Jungle Book', which includes the famous stories of Mowgli, Baloo the bear, and Bagheera the panther.
questions.funFact: Rudyard Kipling was the first English-language writer to receive the Nobel Prize in Literature, and he was also the youngest at that time!

questions.question: Who wrote 'Captain Underpants'?
questions.explanation: Dav Pilkey wrote the 'Captain Underpants' series about two boys who hypnotize their principal into becoming a superhero.
questions.funFact: Dav Pilkey created Captain Underpants when he was in second grade! His teacher told him he would never make a living drawing silly comics.

questions.question: Who wrote 'Charlotte's Web'?
questions.explanation: E.B. White wrote 'Charlotte's Web', a beautiful story about the friendship between a pig named Wilbur and a spider named Charlotte.
questions.funFact: E.B. White got the idea for Charlotte's Web by watching a spider spin a web in his barn on his farm in Maine!

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Hobbit'?
questions.explanation: J.R.R. Tolkien wrote 'The Hobbit', an adventure about a hobbit named Bilbo Baggins who goes on a quest with dwarves and a wizard.
questions.funFact: Tolkien invented entire languages for the elves and other creatures in his books, including alphabets and grammar rules!

questions.question: Sudha Murty wrote which of these books?
options: The Magic Drum and Other Favourite Stories
questions.explanation: Sudha Murty wrote 'The Magic Drum and Other Favourite Stories', a collection of Indian folktales retold for children.
questions.funFact: Sudha Murty writes in both English and Kannada and has donated crores of rupees to build libraries and schools across India!

questions.question: Who wrote 'Pippi Longstocking'?
questions.explanation: Astrid Lindgren wrote 'Pippi Longstocking', about the adventures of a super-strong girl who lives alone with a monkey and a horse.
questions.funFact: Astrid Lindgren first made up Pippi Longstocking stories to tell her daughter when she was sick in bed!

questions.question: Who wrote 'Treasure Island'?
questions.explanation: Robert Louis Stevenson wrote 'Treasure Island', a thrilling pirate adventure about a boy named Jim Hawkins who finds a treasure map.
questions.funFact: Robert Louis Stevenson drew a watercolour map of an imaginary island with his stepson, and that map inspired the entire story!

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Wonderful Wizard of Oz'?
questions.explanation: L. Frank Baum wrote 'The Wonderful Wizard of Oz', the story of Dorothy and her dog Toto who get swept away to the magical Land of Oz.
questions.funFact: L. Frank Baum said the name 'Oz' came to him when he looked at his filing cabinet and saw the drawer labelled 'O-Z'!

questions.question: Who wrote 'The BFG'?
questions.explanation: Roald Dahl wrote 'The BFG' (The Big Friendly Giant), about a kind giant who catches and delivers good dreams to children.
questions.funFact: Roald Dahl dedicated 'The BFG' to his daughter Olivia, and the character Sophie was named after his granddaughter!

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Wind in the Willows'?
questions.explanation: Kenneth Grahame wrote 'The Wind in the Willows', a story about the adventures of Mole, Rat, Toad, and Badger along the river bank.
questions.funFact: Kenneth Grahame first told these stories as bedtime tales for his son, and later wrote them down as letters!

questions.question: Who wrote 'Gitanjali', a collection of poems that won the Nobel Prize?
questions.explanation: Rabindranath Tagore wrote 'Gitanjali' (Song Offerings), a collection of poems. He became the first non-European to win the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1913.
questions.funFact: Tagore also composed the national anthems of two countries: India ('Jana Gana Mana') and Bangladesh ('Amar Sonar Bangla')!

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Adventures of Tom Sawyer'?
questions.explanation: Mark Twain wrote 'The Adventures of Tom Sawyer', about a mischievous boy growing up along the Mississippi River in America.
questions.funFact: Mark Twain's real name was Samuel Clemens. 'Mark Twain' was a riverboat term meaning the water was two fathoms deep!

questions.question: Premchand is best known for writing which novel?
questions.explanation: Munshi Premchand wrote 'Godan' (The Gift of a Cow), considered one of the greatest Hindi novels ever written, about the struggles of an Indian farmer.
questions.funFact: Premchand is called the 'Emperor of Hindi Literature' and wrote over 300 short stories during his career!

questions.question: Who wrote 'Romeo and Juliet'?
questions.explanation: William Shakespeare wrote 'Romeo and Juliet', one of the most famous love stories ever written, set in Verona, Italy.
questions.funFact: Shakespeare invented over 1,700 words we still use today, including 'eyeball', 'bedroom', and 'lonely'!

questions.question: Who wrote 'Oliver Twist'?
questions.explanation: Charles Dickens wrote 'Oliver Twist', a novel about an orphan boy's life on the streets of London.
questions.funFact: Charles Dickens had to work in a shoe polish factory as a child when his father went to prison for debt, which inspired many of his stories!

questions.question: Who wrote 'Pride and Prejudice'?
questions.explanation: Jane Austen wrote 'Pride and Prejudice', a novel about Elizabeth Bennet and Mr. Darcy set in early 19th century England.
questions.funFact: Jane Austen started writing 'Pride and Prejudice' when she was only 21 years old, and she published all her books anonymously as 'A Lady'!

questions.question: Rabindranath Tagore wrote which of these?
questions.explanation: Rabindranath Tagore wrote 'The Home and the World' (Ghare-Baire), a novel set during the Swadeshi movement in Bengal.
questions.funFact: Tagore gave up his knighthood in 1919 as a protest against the Jallianwala Bagh massacre!

questions.question: Who wrote 'A Midsummer Night's Dream'?
questions.explanation: William Shakespeare wrote 'A Midsummer Night's Dream', a comedy about fairies, magic, and mixed-up lovers in an enchanted forest.
questions.funFact: Nobody knows Shakespeare's exact birthday, but it is traditionally celebrated on 23rd April, which is also the date he died!

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Guide'?
questions.explanation: R.K. Narayan wrote 'The Guide', a novel about a tour guide named Raju who is mistaken for a holy man. It won the Sahitya Akademi Award.
questions.funFact: R.K. Narayan never learned to drive a car or ride a bicycle. He walked everywhere in Mysore, which gave him ideas for his stories!

questions.question: Who wrote 'A Christmas Carol'?
questions.explanation: Charles Dickens wrote 'A Christmas Carol', the story of the grumpy Ebenezer Scrooge who is visited by three ghosts on Christmas Eve.
questions.funFact: Dickens wrote 'A Christmas Carol' in just six weeks, and it was so popular that it helped revive the celebration of Christmas in England!

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn'?
questions.explanation: Mark Twain wrote 'The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn', often called one of the greatest American novels, about a boy's journey down the Mississippi River.
questions.funFact: Mark Twain's house had one of the first telephones ever installed in a private home, and he hated it because people kept calling him!

questions.question: Sarojini Naidu, known as the 'Nightingale of India', was famous for which type of writing?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu was a celebrated Indian poet who wrote beautiful poems in English. Her collections include 'The Golden Threshold' and 'The Bird of Time'.
questions.funFact: Sarojini Naidu was also a freedom fighter and became the first woman Governor of an Indian state (Uttar Pradesh)!
